data.title: XLM may hit $0.27 in 2 weeks, facing resistance at $0.25 - risk to $0.22 support zone. Bullish trend predicted.
data.description: Predictions suggest that the price of XLM could potentially rise to a resistance level of $0.27 in the next 14 days. However, there is a significant barrier at the $0.25 pivot point, with bearish pressure posing a threat to push the price down to the support zone at $0.22.

data.title: Tracebit launches community edition for security canary detection, making it easier to deploy canaries at scale.
data.description: In London, the cloud-native deception platform Tracebit has introduced its 'community edition' to promote the idea that security canaries should be a foundational element in security strategies. Canaries, which are decoy resources, serve as early indicators of cyber threats, alerting organizations when malicious actors breach systems and facilitating quick responses. Tracebit, established in 2023, aims to address the challenges associated with implementing and managing canaries on a large scale. The platform's community edition launch emphasizes the importance of integrating canaries into security programs to enhance protection against cyber threats.

data.title: UMC and Polar partner for U.S. onshore semiconductor manufacturing. Expanding with scalable 8-inch high-quality wafer production.
data.description: Hsinchu, Taiwan and Bloomington, Minnesota - United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC), a prominent global semiconductor foundry, and Polar Semiconductor, LLC, a U.S.-based foundry focusing on high-voltage, power, and sensor technologies, have revealed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) for potential collaboration. The agreement aims to investigate working together to establish scalable 8-inch wafer production in the U.S., catering to vital requirements for various applications. UMC is listed on NYSE: UMC and TWSE: 2303, while Polar Semiconductor, LLC operates in Bloomington, Minnesota.

data.title: FICO report shows UK credit card delinquency rising in Sept 2025, especially among long-term cardholders.
data.description: Recent credit card data analysis released by FICO for September 2025 highlights the financial challenges households are grappling with. Following the Autumn Budget, there is a noticeable trend of cardholders with significant debt (three missed payments) slipping into further delinquency. This phenomenon is particularly prevalent among customers who have held their credit card for over five years. The analysis showcases a substantial year-on-year decrease in...

data.title: Al-Futtaim and SNP convert 16 TB ECC to S/4HANA in record time in August 2025 as part of RISE with SAP program.
data.description: In August 2025, Al-Futtaim (AFG) Automotive achieved a successful go-live for their intricate 16 terabyte ECC to S/4HANA transformation project. This initiative was conducted under the RISE with SAP program, with SNP's Kyano CrystalBridge Transformation DTS technology facilitating the conversion of various clients and business modules.

data.title: TRON aims for $0.33 breakout by January 2025 with 17% potential upside and key support at $0.27 amid bullish momentum shift.
data.description: Analysis of TRON's technical indicators indicates a bullish MACD divergence, suggesting a potential upward movement towards a resistance level of $0.33. Price forecasts for TRX anticipate a 17% increase, with significant support identified at $0.27.

Former WTO chief Pascal Lamy has told CGTN Europe that it is unrealistic for France to have a relationship with China separate from the China-EU dialogue. “The US and China have both an economic, a strategic, a tech leadership which we do not have...So we need to catch up.”

data.title: Smartstream launches Agentic AI 'Smart Agents' for enhancing investigations and exceptions handling in financial services.
data.description: In London, Smartstream has unveiled their latest innovation in the form of Agentic AI 'Smart Agents' designed for investigations and exceptions handling. This advanced technology is set to revolutionize the industry with its capabilities. The Smart Agents are equipped to significantly enhance efficiency and accuracy, offering a groundbreaking approach to these processes. With the introduction of this cutting-edge solution, Smartstream aims to streamline operations and boost productivity. Keep an eye on this development as it is expected to make a significant impact in the sector.

data.title: Vow ASA and Scanship win contract worth EUR 29.6 million for equipment deliveries to two newbuilds from a European shipyard; deliveries to start in July 2027.
data.description: In Oslo on December 4, 2025, Vow ASA (listed as OSE: VOW) and its subsidiary Scanship have secured a significant purchase order from a leading European shipyard totaling EUR 29.6 million. The delivery of equipment is set to commence in July 2027 for the initial vessel and will extend into the year 2028.

data.title: Educators in the UK are concerned about the readiness of young people for an AI future, according to Pearson School Report 2025.
data.description: On December 4, 2025, in London, a study conducted by Pearson (FTSE: PSON.L), a prominent global education company, indicated a rising worry among educators regarding the readiness of young individuals in the UK for an AI-centric future. The research findings from the Pearson School Report 2025 highlight this issue, raising pertinent concerns within the educational community. The report presents data showing a growing apprehension about the adequacy of preparations for the evolving landscape influenced by artificial intelligence.

data.title: Festi hf. issues new bill FESTI260616 for ISK 1,300 million at 7.94% interest, to be traded on Nasdaq Iceland.
data.description: The company Festi hf. has successfully sold a new six-month bill, named FESTI260616, with a nominal value of ISK 1,300 million. The bill was sold at terms that entail a 7.94% flat interest rate. It is anticipated that the bill will be accepted for trading and included in the listings on Nasdaq Iceland.

data.title: Dubai ranks 12th in Top 100 City Destinations for 2025; Paris tops list with 18 million visitors.
data.description: In Dubai, according to data from Euromonitor International, Paris has been declared the top global city for tourism for the fifth consecutive year. The city welcomed more than 18 million international visitors in 2025, reinforcing its status with its robust tourism facilities, rich cultural offerings, and commitment to sustainability. Euromonitor International’s Top 100 City Destinations Index 2025 unveiled this information, highlighting Paris's continued appeal among travelers worldwide.
